class.name: FlecsWorld
class.brief_description: The main ECS world node that integrates Flecs with Godot.
class.description: [b]FlecsWorld[/b] is a Godot node that provides access to a Flecs Entity Component System (ECS) world. It enables data-oriented design patterns in Godot, allowing you to efficiently manage large numbers of entities and components through declarative systems. The world can be configured to progress during rendering, physics, or manually. Components and systems are defined through C++ code, GDScript integration, or Flecs script files. Entities can be created, destroyed, and queried at runtime, with full support for prefabs and event-driven systems.

signal.name: stagehand_signal_emitted
signal.description: Emitted when a Stagehand event is forwarded into Flecs. The signal carries the event name, an optional payload [param data], and the optional [param source_entity_id] of the originating entity (0 indicates an internal emitter).

method.name: add_component
method.description: Adds a component (or tag) to an entity by name. If the component is a tag (has no data), this registers the tag on the entity. For components with data, use [method set_component] instead.

method.name: create_entity
method.description: Creates a new entity and returns its unique ID. Optionally assign a name for easier lookup via [method lookup].

method.name: destroy_entity
method.description: Destroys an entity and removes it from the world.

method.name: emit_event
method.description: Emits a Stagehand event payload into Flecs. Event data is stored in an [code]EventPayload[/code] component that is available to Flecs systems. If [param source_entity_id] is 0, an internal emitter entity is used.

method.name: enable_entity
method.description: Enables or disables an entity. Disabled entities are skipped by systems but remain in the world.

method.name: get_component
method.description: Gets a component value from an entity. Returns the component data as a Variant. If the entity doesn't have the component, returns null.

method.name: get_entity_name
method.description: Gets the name assigned to an entity.

method.name: get_modules_to_import
method.description: Returns the list of Flecs modules configured for import at startup.

method.name: get_progress_tick
method.description: Returns the current progress tick mode.

method.name: get_world_configuration
method.description: Gets the world configuration singleton as a dictionary.

method.name: has_component
method.description: Checks if an entity has a component.

method.name: instantiate_prefab
method.description: Instantiates a prefab by name and returns the entity ID of the new instance. Component data can be overridden via the [param components] dictionary. Returns 0 if the prefab doesn't exist.

method.name: is_alive
method.description: Checks if an entity is alive (exists in the world).

method.name: lookup
method.description: Looks up an entity by name and returns its ID, or 0 if not found.

method.name: progress
method.description: Advances the ECS world by a delta time. Called automatically based on [member progress_tick], but can also be called manually from GDScript.

method.name: remove_component
method.description: Removes a component (or tag) from an entity.

method.name: run_system
method.description: Runs a specific system manually. The [param system] argument can be either a system ID (int) or name (String). Optionally pass [param parameters] to the system. Useful for triggering on-demand systems from GDScript.

method.name: set_component
method.description: Sets a component value for an entity. If the entity doesn't have the component, it is added first.

method.name: set_modules_to_import
method.description: Sets the list of Flecs modules (library names) to import on startup.

method.name: set_progress_tick
method.description: Sets when (or if) the world progresses automatically.

method.name: set_world_configuration
method.description: Sets the world configuration singleton. Configuration is typically used by systems at startup.

members.member: The list of Flecs modules (library names) imported during world initialization.
Controls when the world progresses automatically: rendering tick, physics tick, or manual progression.
Global world configuration data exposed to ECS systems.

constants.constant: The world progresses in [method _physics_process] (fixed-rate physics tick).
No automatic world progression; the world must be progressed manually via [method progress].
